data.title: Sprinklr, Inc. Earnings Call: Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Sprinklr, Inc. ((CXM)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Sprinklr, Inc.’s recent earnings call painted a mixed picture, showcasing both strengths and challenges. The company reported robust revenue growth and successful customer initiatives, yet faced ongoing hurdles in subscription renewals and service implementations. While strategic investments and transformations are underway, their full impact remains to be seen.

Revenue Growth

Sprinklr reported a 9% year-over-year increase in total revenue for the third quarter, reaching $219.1 million. Subscription revenue also saw a rise, growing by 5% to $190.3 million. This growth underscores the company’s ability to expand its market presence despite challenging conditions.

Non-GAAP Operating Income

The company achieved a non-GAAP operating income of $33.5 million, resulting in a 15% non-GAAP operating margin for the quarter. This reflects Sprinklr’s focus on maintaining profitability while navigating market dynamics.

Project Bearhug Success

Project Bearhug has shown promising early results, strengthening C-suite relationships and aligning more closely with customer priorities. This initiative has been pivotal in improving renewal rates, setting a positive trajectory into fiscal year 2027.

Customer Success Stories

A notable highlight was an expansion deal with a leading Latin American bank, which led to significant productivity boosts and improvements in customer satisfaction (CSAT). Such success stories exemplify Sprinklr’s ability to deliver value to its clients.

Improved Financial Outlook

Sprinklr has raised its expectations for both subscription and total revenue estimates for fiscal year 2026. The company now anticipates total revenue to be between $853 million and $854 million, reflecting confidence in its growth strategy.

Subscription Revenue Challenges

Despite a 5% increase in subscription revenue, Sprinklr faces downward pressure from renewals, a challenge persisting for over two years. Addressing these renewal challenges remains a priority for the company.

Customer Cohort Decline

The third quarter saw a decline in the number of customers contributing $1 million or more in subscription revenue, decreasing by four from the previous quarter. This highlights the need for Sprinklr to enhance customer retention strategies.

High Data and Hosting Costs

Sprinklr is experiencing increased data and hosting costs, driven by business opportunities and the expansion of AI capabilities. Managing these costs is crucial for sustaining profitability.

Challenges in Implementation

The company acknowledged challenges in some service implementations, with inconsistencies in execution being addressed. Efforts are underway to streamline processes and improve service delivery.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Sprinklr’s guidance for the fourth quarter and full fiscal year reflects a positive outlook, with projected Q4 revenue between $216.5 million and $217.5 million, indicating a 7% growth year over year. The company also expects subscription revenue to rise by 5%. For the fiscal year, total revenue is anticipated to be between $853 million and $854 million, with a focus on improving renewal rates and investing in AI and R&D to drive future growth.

In summary, Sprinklr’s earnings call highlighted a blend of achievements and challenges. The company is on a growth trajectory with strong revenue figures and strategic initiatives like Project Bearhug. However, it must address subscription renewal pressures and implementation challenges to sustain its momentum.

data.title: Okta, Inc. Earnings Call Highlights Strong Growth and AI Focus
data.description:
Okta, Inc. ((OKTA)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Okta, Inc. Reports Strong Quarter with Promising Growth Opportunities

The recent earnings call of Okta, Inc. reflected a strong quarter with significant contributions from new products and a positive financial outlook. The company is focusing on AI security, which presents a promising growth opportunity. However, there is some uncertainty due to limited guidance for FY ’27 and potential risks associated with AI agent security. Overall, the sentiment is positive with optimism for future growth.

Strong Financial Performance

Okta reported another solid quarter, showcasing improvements in sales productivity and positive trends in key performance indicators. The company maintains a strong balance sheet with nearly $2.5 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments, underscoring its financial robustness.

Growth in New Products

Okta’s new products, including Okta Identity Governance and Okta for AI agents, have significantly contributed to the company’s results. A record number of customers are engaging with these solutions, highlighting the successful integration and market acceptance of these offerings.

AI Security as a Growth Catalyst

Focusing on securing AI agents, Okta sees this as a significant new opportunity and a catalyst for growth. The company has already engaged with over 100 customers, representing over $200 million in existing annual recurring revenue (ARR), indicating strong interest and potential in this area.

Positive Outlook for Revenue Growth

For FY ’26, Okta expects total revenue growth of 11%, a non-GAAP operating margin of 26%, and a free cash flow margin of approximately 29%. This positive outlook reflects the company’s confidence in its strategic initiatives and market positioning.

Sales Specialization Strategy Success

The specialization strategy in sales is yielding positive results for Okta, with improvements in account executive productivity, tenure, and low attrition rates. This strategy is proving effective in enhancing sales efficiency and effectiveness.

Limited Guidance for FY ’27

Due to the importance of Q4, Okta did not provide guidance for FY ’27, creating some uncertainty regarding future financial performance. Investors and analysts will be keenly awaiting the next quarterly earnings call for further insights.

Potential Risks with AI Agent Security

While there is a surge in interest for AI agent security, it is still early days. The market is dynamic, and there is a risk of being locked into a single platform, which could pose challenges as the landscape evolves.

Forward-Looking Guidance

In the third quarter of fiscal 2026 earnings webcast, Okta provided detailed guidance about its financial outlook and market positioning. The company anticipates a total revenue growth of 10% for Q4 and FY 2026, with current RPO growth projected at 9%. Okta expects a non-GAAP operating margin of 25% for Q4 and a free cash flow margin of approximately 31%. For the full year FY 2026, Okta has raised its outlook, now predicting an 11% total revenue growth, a 26% non-GAAP operating margin, and approximately 29% free cash flow margin. The company plans to issue FY 2027 guidance in the next quarterly earnings call.

In conclusion, Okta, Inc.’s earnings call highlighted a strong quarter with positive sentiment and optimism for future growth. The company’s focus on AI security and new product growth are promising, though some uncertainty remains due to limited future guidance and potential risks in AI agent security. Investors should watch for further updates in the next earnings call.

data.title: GitLab’s Earnings Call: Strong Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Gitlab, Inc. Class A ((GTLB)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

GitLab’s recent earnings call painted a picture of robust growth and strategic advancements, despite some challenges in specific sectors. The sentiment was largely positive, with strong revenue growth and operating margin improvements leading the way. The company’s focus on expanding GitLab Ultimate and the development of the Duo Agent platform were highlighted as key drivers of success, although there were notable challenges in the US public sector and SMB market. Overall, the positives in revenue and strategic growth initiatives outweighed the negatives, indicating a promising outlook for GitLab.

Strong Revenue Growth

GitLab reported a remarkable 25% year-over-year increase in revenue, reaching $244 million, which exceeded their Q3 guidance by two points. This strong performance underscores the company’s ability to capture market share and drive financial success, even amidst challenging market conditions.

Increased Operating Margin

The company’s non-GAAP operating margin reached an impressive 18%, a full five points above the Q3 guidance. This improvement reflects GitLab’s effective cost management strategies and operational efficiencies, contributing to its overall financial health.

Growth in GitLab Ultimate

GitLab Ultimate now accounts for 54% of total Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR), marking significant expansions at major client organizations. This growth highlights the increasing adoption of GitLab’s premium offerings and its ability to deliver value to large enterprises.

AI Platform Growth

The GitLab Duo Agent platform is on track for general availability, showing promising early results and expansions even before its official launch. This platform is expected to play a crucial role in GitLab’s strategic shift towards a hybrid seat plus usage-based business model, driven by AI advancements.

Recognition and Leadership

GitLab’s leadership in the industry was reaffirmed as it was named a leader in the 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant for DevOps platforms for the third consecutive year, and for AI coding assistance for the second year. This recognition underscores GitLab’s innovation and influence in the tech sector.

Financial Stability

Ending the quarter with $1.2 billion in cash and investments, GitLab demonstrated a strong financial position. This financial stability provides a solid foundation for continued investment in growth and innovation.

Softness in US Public Sector

The US public sector presented challenges due to slower decision-making related to the government shutdown, creating headwinds that impacted GitLab’s performance in this segment.

SMB Market Weakness

Persistent softness in the SMB segment, which accounts for roughly 8% of ARR, continued to impact GitLab’s growth. This segment’s challenges highlight the need for targeted strategies to address market-specific issues.

Challenges with First Order Business

While there was a slight improvement in the mix of first order versus expansion, it remains below desired levels. GitLab recognizes the need for further investment and time to optimize this aspect of its business model.

Forward-Looking Guidance

GitLab provided optimistic guidance for the future, expecting fourth-quarter revenue between $251 million and $252 million, reflecting a 19% year-over-year growth. For the full fiscal year 2026, they anticipate total revenue of $946 million to $947 million, representing a 25% growth. The company is shifting towards a hybrid seat plus usage-based business model with the Duo Agent platform, aiming to capture more value from increased software activity driven by AI. Despite challenges, GitLab remains confident in its long-term growth prospects.

In conclusion, GitLab’s earnings call highlighted a strong performance with significant revenue growth and strategic advancements, particularly in AI and premium offerings. While challenges exist in certain sectors, the company’s financial stability and forward-looking strategies position it well for continued success. Investors and market watchers can remain optimistic about GitLab’s trajectory, as it continues to innovate and expand its market presence.

data.title: Inditex Earnings Call: Strong Growth Amid Challenges
data.description:
Industria De Diseno Textil, S.A. Unsponsored Adr ((IDEXY)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The recent earnings call for Industria De Diseno Textil, S.A. Unsponsored Adr (Inditex) conveyed a positive sentiment, underscored by strong sales growth and profit improvements despite facing currency headwinds. The company demonstrated confidence in its future growth through global expansion and investments in logistics and technology. While challenges such as currency fluctuations and potential EU legislation were acknowledged, they did not overshadow the overall positive performance.

Strong Sales Growth

Sales in constant currency increased by 6.2% over the first nine months of 2025, with a notable 4.9% growth in the third quarter despite currency headwinds. Furthermore, store and online sales between November 1st and December 1st surged by 10.6%, showcasing the company’s robust market presence and consumer demand.

Profit and Margin Improvements

Inditex reported a 3.6% increase in profit before tax, reaching EUR 6 billion, and a 3.9% rise in net income to EUR 4.6 billion. The gross margin improved to 59.7%, supported by an operating leverage of 29 basis points over nine months, indicating efficient cost management and profitability.

Global Expansion and Market Penetration

The company expanded its footprint by opening stores in 39 markets, including new entries such as Bershka in Denmark and Oysho’s expansion in Europe. Inditex continues to operate in 214 markets, highlighting significant growth opportunities and its commitment to global market penetration.

Diversification and Resilience

Inditex’s diversification across concepts, channels, geographies, and sourcing markets enhances its business model resilience. This strategy allows the company to mitigate risks and capitalize on various market opportunities, contributing to its sustained performance.

Logistics and Technology Investments

A 2-year logistics expansion plan is underway, with a EUR 1.8 billion investment aimed at increasing capacities and efficiency. The rollout of new security technology across concepts further underscores Inditex’s commitment to operational excellence and innovation.

Currency Headwinds

Sales growth was impacted by approximately 350 basis points due to currency headwinds, with an anticipated minus 4% top-line currency impact for the full year 2025. Despite this challenge, the company remains focused on its growth trajectory.

Potential EU Legislation Impact

Concerns were raised regarding potential EU legislation to remove duties exemptions on low-value parcels. However, Inditex clarified that it does not rely on de minimis rules in its operations, suggesting minimal impact from such regulatory changes.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Inditex’s 9-month 2025 results presentation highlighted a robust financial performance, with sales in constant currency reaching EUR 28.2 billion. The company reported a stable gross margin guidance for 2025, emphasizing reinvestment in efficiencies to maintain competitive differentiation. The presentation also underscored strong store and online sales growth and ongoing investments in logistics and store network expansion.

In conclusion, the earnings call for Inditex reflected a positive outlook, driven by strong sales growth, profit improvements, and strategic investments. Despite challenges such as currency headwinds and potential regulatory changes, the company’s global expansion and diversification strategies position it well for continued success.

data.title: Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR’s Optimistic Earnings Call Overview
data.description:
Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR ((HERE)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR’s recent earnings call painted a picture of optimism and growth, despite some challenges. The company reported strong revenue and margin growth, successful strategic partnerships, and international expansion. However, it also acknowledged a net loss and previous supply chain issues. Overall, the sentiment was positive, with ongoing improvements anticipated.

Strong Revenue Growth

Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR reported a significant increase in total revenue, reaching RMB 127.1 million. This growth was largely driven by the pop toy business, which saw a 93.3% increase quarter-over-quarter from RMB 65.8 million, surpassing previous guidance.

Gross Margin Improvement

The company’s gross margins expanded to 41.2%, up from 34.7% in the previous quarter. This improvement reflects enhanced profitability and operational efficiency.

Successful Strategic Partnerships

Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR established strategic partnerships with Beijing Radio and Television Station, participated in the China Open, and collaborated with top entertainment shows and urban development projects, strengthening its market presence.

International Market Expansion

The company made significant strides in international markets, establishing distribution networks in around 20 countries, including North America, Europe, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East.

Operational Efficiency

Operating expenses as a percentage of revenue decreased, with sales and marketing expenses dropping to 21.7% from 29% and R&D expenses to 12.5% from 13.5%, indicating improved operational efficiency.

Net Loss from Continuing Operations

Despite the positive developments, Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR reported a net loss from continuing operations of RMB 25.8 million, compared with RMB 21.8 million in the previous quarter.

Capacity and Supply Chain Challenges

The company faced previous supply chain shortages and capacity issues, which impacted its ability to meet demand. Efforts are underway to increase production capacity to address these challenges.

Forward-Looking Guidance

Looking ahead, Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR anticipates revenues in the range of RMB 150 million to RMB 160 million for the second quarter and RMB 750 million to RMB 800 million for the full fiscal year 2026. This forecast is driven by robust product launches and expanded production capacity.

In conclusion, Quantasing Group Ltd. ADR’s earnings call reflected a positive outlook, with strong revenue growth, improved margins, and successful strategic partnerships. Despite challenges such as a net loss and supply chain issues, the company is poised for continued growth and expansion in both domestic and international markets.

data.title: Marvell Technology’s Earnings Call Highlights Record Growth
data.description:
Marvell Technology Group ((MRVL)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The recent earnings call for Marvell Technology Group was marked by a largely positive sentiment, underscored by record-breaking revenue and robust growth in key segments such as data center and communications. The strategic acquisition of Celestial AI is anticipated to further enhance Marvell’s industry standing. Despite facing challenges like a sequential decline in custom revenue and seasonal downturns in the consumer business, the company’s strong financial performance and optimistic outlook for FY2027 were the focal points of the discussion.

Record-Breaking Revenue Growth

Marvell Technology Group reported a record revenue of $2.075 billion for Q3 FY2026, marking a 3% sequential increase and an impressive 37% year-over-year growth. This significant achievement highlights the company’s strong market position and effective growth strategies.

Strategic Acquisition of Celestial AI

The acquisition of Celestial AI was a key highlight of the earnings call. This strategic move is expected to bolster Marvell’s data center portfolio, allowing the company to capitalize on opportunities within accelerated infrastructure and further solidify its competitive edge.

Data Center Segment Surge

The data center segment experienced a remarkable 38% year-over-year growth, driven by strong demand for AI solutions. This segment is projected to continue its upward trajectory, with an expected 25% year-over-year growth in FY2027, showcasing Marvell’s strength in this critical area.

Strong Fiscal 2027 Outlook

Looking ahead, Marvell anticipates data center revenue to grow by more than 25% in FY2027, with overall company revenue forecasted to reach $2.2 billion for Q4 FY2026. This optimistic outlook reflects the company’s confidence in sustaining its growth momentum.

Strong Financial Performance

Marvell’s financial performance was robust, with non-GAAP earnings per share of $0.76, reflecting a 77% year-over-year growth. The company also reported a record cash flow from operations of $582 million, underscoring its financial health and operational efficiency.

Sequential Decline in Custom Revenue

Despite the positive overall performance, the custom revenue segment saw a sequential decline in demand. However, Marvell expects growth in this area to rebound in the coming quarters, indicating potential for recovery.

Steep Seasonal Declines in Consumer Business

The consumer business segment is anticipated to experience steep seasonal declines, which may impact the overall performance of the communications end market. This challenge highlights the need for strategic adjustments to mitigate seasonal impacts.

Guidance and Future Outlook

Marvell’s guidance for the future remains optimistic, with a forecast of $2.2 billion in total company revenue for Q4 FY2026. The company expects continued growth into fiscal 2027 and beyond, driven by strong performance in the data center business and other key segments.

In conclusion, Marvell Technology Group’s earnings call painted a picture of a company on a strong growth trajectory, buoyed by record-breaking revenue and strategic initiatives like the acquisition of Celestial AI. While challenges remain, particularly in the custom revenue and consumer business segments, the overall sentiment was positive, with a promising outlook for the future.

data.title: ALT5 Sigma Faces Nasdaq Compliance Deadline
data.description:
TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The latest announcement is out from ALT5 Sigma Corporation ( (ALTS) ).

On December 2, 2025, ALT5 Sigma Corporation announced it received a notification from Nasdaq regarding a delay in filing its Form 10-Q for the period ending September 27, 2025. This delay means the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq’s listing requirements, but it has until January 20, 2026, to submit a compliance plan. If accepted, ALT5 may be granted an extension to regain compliance by May 18, 2026. The notification does not immediately affect the trading of ALT5’s shares, and the company is working to complete the filing within the required timeframe.

Spark’s Take on ALTS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ALTS is a Neutral.

ALT5 Sigma Corporation’s stock score of 46 reflects its financial challenges, including profitability issues and a leveraged balance sheet, despite recent revenue growth and positive cash flow generation. Technical analysis indicates mixed short-term momentum with some long-term strength, while the valuation is unattractive due to a negative P/E ratio.

To see Spark’s full report on ALTS stock, click here.

More about ALT5 Sigma Corporation

ALT5 Sigma Corporation is a fintech company specializing in digital asset treasury strategies, leveraging blockchain infrastructure to optimize operations. Founded in 2018, ALT5 has processed over $5 billion in cryptocurrency transactions and focuses on expanding the $WLFI ecosystem across retail platforms, payment integrations, and international markets.

Hooker Furniture ( (HOFT) ) has issued an update.

On December 1, 2025, Hooker Furnishings Corporation announced an agreement to sell its Pulaski Furniture and Samuel Lawrence Furniture brands to Magnussen Home Furnishings, Inc. for an estimated $4.8 million, subject to adjustments. This move is part of Hooker’s strategy to streamline its portfolio and focus on more profitable brands. The sale will also relieve Hooker of $4.8 million in showroom lease liabilities as Magnussen takes over the lease of the High Point showroom. The transaction is expected to close by mid-December 2025, with Hooker retaining the Samuel Lawrence Hospitality brand for its hospitality business. This strategic decision is aimed at enhancing shareholder value and improving profitability.

Hooker Furnishings Corporation is a designer, marketer, and importer of casegoods, leather furniture, fabric-upholstered furniture, lighting, accessories, and home décor for residential, hospitality, and contract markets. The company also manufactures premium residential custom leather and fabric-upholstered furniture and outdoor furniture domestically. Major product categories include home entertainment, home office, accent, dining, and bedroom furniture, sold under the Hooker Furniture brand. Hooker Furnishings operates distribution centers in Virginia, North Carolina, and Vietnam, with corporate offices and manufacturing facilities in Virginia, North Carolina, and California.
